title: Nozzle and closure assembly
abstract: A nozzle and closure system (10) for a bottle (16), the system comprising a nozzle (12) configured to be received by the neck (40) of a bottle (16) and to be secured therein, and a closure (14) configured to engage with the outer surface of the neck (40) of the bottle (16) and to close the nozzle (12). The nozzle (12) and the closure (14) are configured to cooperate so that the nozzle (12) is retained within the closure (14). Also disclosed is a method of assembling a bottle (16) comprising a nozzle (12) and a closure (14), the method comprising providing a nozzle and closure system (10), wherein the nozzle (12) is retained within the closure (14), and then mounting the nozzle and closure system (10) on the neck (40) of a bottle (16).
claims: Claims 1. A nozzle and closure system for a bottle, the system comprising: a nozzle configured to be received by the neck of a bottle and to be secured therein, and a closure configured to engage with the outer surface of the neck of the bottle and to close the nozzle ; wherein the nozzle and the closure are configured to cooperate so that the nozzle is retained within the closure. 2. A nozzle and closure system according to claim 1 , wherein the nozzle comprises a nozzle retention feature configured to retain the nozzle within the closure . 3. A nozzle and closure system according to claim 1 , wherein the closure comprises a closure retention feature configured to retain the nozzle within the closure. 4. A nozzle and closure system according to claim 1 , wherein the nozzle comprises a nozzle retention feature and the closure comprises a closure retention feature, wherein the nozzle retention feature and the closure retention feature are configured to cooperate to retain the nozzle within the closure. 5. A nozzle and closure system according to claim 4, wherein the nozzle retention feature comprises a circumferenti al ridge that protrudes outwardly from the nozzle. 6. A nozzle and closure system according to claim 4 or 5, wherein the nozzle comprises a flange configured to be seated on the rim of the bottle when the nozzle is inserted into and secured in the neck of the bottle. 7. A nozzle and closure system according claim 6, wherein the closure comprises an abutment surface configured to abut the upper surface of the nozzle flange and to apply downward pressure to the nozzle via the nozzle flange as the nozzle and closure system is mounted on the bottle. 8. A nozzle and closure system according to claim 6 or claim 7, wherein the nozzle retention feature is formed on the flange. 9. A nozzle and closure system according to claim 6 or claim 7, wherein the flange is the nozzle retention feature. 10. A nozzle and closure system according to any of claims 4 to 9, wherein the closure retention feature comprises a protrusion on the inner surface of the closure. 1 1. A nozzle and closure system according to claim 10, wherein the closure retention feature comprises a plurality of protrusions on the inner surface of the closure. 12. A nozzle and closure system according to claim 1 1 , wherein the plurality of protrusions are arranged on the inner surface of the closure to form a discontinuous ring of protrusions. 13. A nozzle and closure system according to claim 10, wherein the protrusion is in the form of a circumferenti al ridge extending around an inner circumference of the closure. 14. A nozzle and closure system according to any of claims 4 to 13, wherein the closure retention feature is configured to extend under the nozzle retention feature, thereby causing the nozzle to be retained within the closure. 15. A nozzle and closure system according to claim 4, wherein the nozzle retention feature is a protrusion and the closure retention feature is a recess configured to receive the nozzle retention feature, thereby causing the nozzle to be retained within the closure; or wherein the closure retention feature is a protrusion and the nozzle retention feature is a recess configured to receive the closure retention feature, thereby causing the nozzle to be retained within the closure . 16. A nozzle and closure system according to any of claims 1 to 4, wherein the nozzle is held within the closure by an interference fit between the nozzle and the closure. 17. A nozzle and closure system according to any preceding claim, wherein the nozzle and closure system is for an ophthalmic dropper bottle. 18. A nozzle and closure system according to any preceding claim, wherein the closure comprises a screw thread on its inner surface so that it may be engaged with the neck of the bottle by screwing the closure onto the neck of the bottle. 19. A nozzle and closure system according to any of claims 1 to 17, wherein the closure is configured to be engaged with the neck of the bottle by a snap fit. 20. A nozzle and closure system according to any preceding claim, wherein the nozzle is retained within the closure in axial alignment with the closure. 21. A nozzle and closure system according to any preceding claim, further comprising the bottle. 22. A nozzle and closure system according to claim 21 , wherein the nozzle is configured to be secured in the neck of the bottle by an interference fit . 23. A nozzle and closure system according to claim 21 , wherein the nozzle comprises a nozzle securing feature configured to cooperate with the neck of the bottle to secure the nozzle in the neck of the bottle. 24. A nozzle and closure system according to claim 23, wherein the nozzle securing feature is a protrusion on the outer surface of the nozzle that is configured to cooperate with a corresponding recess on the inner surface of the neck of the bottle. 25. A nozzle and closure system according to claim 23 or 24, wherein the securing feature extends entirely around the circumference of the nozzle. 26. A nozzle and closure system according to any of claims 21 to 25, wherein the nozzle and closure system is configured so that once the nozzle has been inserted into and secured in the neck of the bottle the closure can be removed from the bottle without removing the nozzle from the neck of the bottle. 27. A nozzle and closure system according to any of claims 21 to 26, wherein the system is configured so that the force required to remove the nozzle from the neck of the bottle is greater than the upward force exerted by the closure on the nozzle as the closure is removed from the bottle. 28. A nozzle and closure system according to any of claims 21 to 27, wherein the nozzle and closure system is configured so that the force of interaction between the nozzle and closure is reduced when the nozzle and closure system is mounted on the bottle compared to when the nozzle and closure system is not mounted on the bottle . 29. A nozzle and closure system according to any of claims 21 to 28, wherein the closure is configured to expand in diameter as it is engaged with the neck of the bottle. 30. A nozzle and closure system according to any preceding claim, wherein the nozzle and closure system is configured so that the nozzle is inserted into and secured in the neck of the bottle as the closure is engaged with the neck of the bottle. 31. A nozzle and closure system according to any preceding claim, wherein the nozzle and closure system is configured such that the nozzle may be retained within the closure in a loose fit. 32. A method of assembling a bottle comprising a nozzle and a closure, the method comprising: providing a nozzle and closure system, wherein the nozzle is retained within the closure; and mounting the nozzle and closure system on the neck of a bottle. 33. A method according to claim 31 , wherein the nozzle and closure system is as described in any of claims 1 to 31. 34. A method according to claim 32 or 33, further comprising assembling a nozzle and a closure to provide the nozzle and closure system. 35. A method according to any of claims 32 to 34, wherein the bottle comprising the nozzle and closure system is an ophthalmic dropper bottle.
